action.hypothesis: Matched architecture probe on `lowrank_gated_residual` with `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=2`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` fixed. Final reconstruction formula is `x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_i alpha_i b_i`, where `U(Vx)` is a static low-rank trunk library and `{b_i}` is a static residual dictionary; the gate only changes how residual coefficients are selected, not the exported decoder basis. This is `扩展兼容`: LUTurbo/Lottable can export it as one static trunk lookup plus one sparse weighted sum over a static residual vector library, with the gate absorbed entirely into online coefficient/selection logic. At EF=2 its encoder selection cost stays within the `≤ 1.5×h×n` hard budget and remains below the current two-stage soft-codebook mainline. The single question is whether this simpler low-rank gated selector is intrinsically strong enough in the current low-EF deployable regime to justify any later follow-up, or whether the mainline’s soft-codebook plus second stage is clearly necessary.
action.summary: Run one valid matched architecture probe on `lowrank_gated_residual` while keeping the `K=128`, `EF=2`, `signum`, `LR=3.0e-3` mainline recipe fixed. This cleanly tests whether a cheaper single-stage low-rank gated architecture is worth any continued attention under LUTurbo-compatible low-EF constraints.
action.self_review: This obeys the single-variable rule by changing only `architecture` and explicitly pinning the current mainline recipe fields that caused recent policy confusion. `lowrank_gated_residual` is the highest-value remaining matched probe because it preserves LUTurbo exportability, stays within the EF=2 selection-cost budget, and tests whether a simpler one-stage low-rank selector can recover more quality than plain `lowrank_residual` without paying the full mainline cost. The main risk is that the added gate branch may not close enough of the FVU gap to justify even its moderate extra runtime.
notes_to_memory: Compatibility: `扩展兼容`. Export path is `U(Vx)` trunk lookup plus one sparse residual lookup over a fixed decoder library; gating affects only selection, not decoder exportability.
This round is a strict matched architecture probe: keep `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=2`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` explicitly pinned, and change only `ARCHITECTURE`.

Judge family worthiness using `tokens_per_sec`, total duration, and `final_total_tokens` alongside final FVU, because a tiny quality gain with noticeably worse runtime would be negative for LUTurbo’s true objective.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_gated_residual` is clearly worse than the mainline in both FVU and runtime metrics, discard it and return to `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` next round.
If it is modestly worse in FVU but materially better in throughput or total runtime, record it as a lower-cost compatible side branch, but still return to the mainline next round per probe policy.
If it is unexpectedly close to the mainline at lower selection cost, schedule at most one later family-specific follow-up after the required return to the mainline.

result.description: Run one valid matched architecture probe on `lowrank_gated_residual` while keeping the `K=128`, `EF=2`, `signum`, `LR=3.0e-3` mainline recipe fixed. This cleanly tests whether a cheaper single-stage low-rank gated architecture is worth any continued attention under LUTurbo-compatible low-EF constraints.
result.self_review: This obeys the single-variable rule by changing only `architecture` and explicitly pinning the current mainline recipe fields that caused recent policy confusion. `lowrank_gated_residual` is the highest-value remaining matched probe because it preserves LUTurbo exportability, stays within the EF=2 selection-cost budget, and tests whether a simpler one-stage low-rank selector can recover more quality than plain `lowrank_residual` without paying the full mainline cost. The main risk is that the added gate branch may not close enough of the FVU gap to justify even its moderate extra runtime.
